goal: Tooling for #11: generate lightweight .h900 card-face thumbnails so the Celtic poster stops OOM-ing on mobile
done: Added tools/gen-card-thumbs.sh — downloads each deck's card faces from the CDN and produces .h<H>.webp (default h900) via cwebp into tools/card-thumbs/assets/<deck>/, ready to upload. Validated: pawbit 01-the-fool-upright 38KB->25KB (604x900); full-res decks drop ~8x decoded memory (17MB->~2MB/card), so 10 Celtic cards go ~170MB->~22MB. gitignored the output dir.
verified: cwebp resize validated on one real card
script not run at full scale (1716 files) — founder runs + uploads
notes: #11 remaining (founder/follow-up): (1) run the script for all decks + upload tools/card-thumbs/assets/ to the CDN preserving paths — speeds daily/question posters too (already prefer .h900, currently fall back to full-res); (2) one-line poster.js change so the CELTIC path also expands its primary via buildPosterCardVariantCandidates (deferred until the reversed-flip worktree branch merges).
